key_insight: LoSA-Net improves 3D MRI prediction of perineural invasion by combining localized attention and scale-adaptive feature mixing to preserve subtle boundary details that standard volumetric models can lose.
clinical_relevance: A more reliable preoperative PNI assessment could help inform surgical planning and risk stratification in cholangiocarcinoma.
method: Talking Neighborhood Attention keeps nerve-aligned local details with head-wise mixing.
Scale-Adaptive Feature Mixing adjusts receptive fields using multi-scale depthwise processing.
Cross-Scale Refinement and Alignment preserves consistency between coarse context and fine boundaries across network stages.
evidence_signal: On contrast-enhanced MRI from 168 cholangiocarcinoma patients, LoSA-Net achieved an AUC of 0.7567 and outperformed representative CNN and transformer baselines under matched settings.
watch_item: The result comes from a single retrospective dataset, so external validation is needed before assuming the performance will generalize.
paper_title: LoSA-Net: A Localized and Scale-Adaptive Network for Boundary-Sensitive Prediction of Perineural Invasion in 3D MRI
